reviewer.displayName: VSBDev (accountable operator) — independent academic review agent

conflictOfInterest.details: This run performed only the academic review role against the frozen candidate. It did not author, revise, or adjudicate the pack, did not read the authoring conversation or any other review, and did not load another EmbeddedKnowledge role skill. The accountable principal github:VSBDev also operates the authoring agent under the disclosed founding-stage single-maintainer allowance; provenance here is disclosed and attested, not verified.

evidenceChecked: lesson.json#scenes (11 scenes, kinds, order, required flags, claimCoverage) and #estimatedMinutes
lesson.json#outcomes and #prerequisites against site/data/premed-graph.json nodes topic-orientation-diagnostic-baseline and topic-orientation-map-and-pathways
course/PREMED-SYLLABUS.md lines 111, 144, 161, 850 (confidence compared with performance; high-confidence error routes to remediation)
research/CONTENT-AUTHORING-EVIDENCE-2026-07-19.md sections 4, 6, 7 and Method-and-limitations (worked examples, expertise reversal, retrieval, spacing)
content/030-concept.md#state-to-route table and the guidance asymmetry paragraph
content/040-worked-example.md#worked-example-three-node-baseline (Alder, Birch, Copper decisions and independent check)
content/090-synthesis.md state/route/do-not-infer table and recovery route
content/100-assessment.md mastery threshold arithmetic (20 of 25; 6 of 10 component minimums)
assessment.json#item-separate-confidence answer key, maxSelections and rubric point split
assessment.json#item-build-baseline stimulus (Cedar, Flint, Harbor) against the operational state definitions
assessment.json#item-transfer-redesign answer logic and rubric criteria
assessment.json#rubrics point sums against item points (2+3+10+10 = 25)
Repository-wide search for a lessons/PREM-LPP-001 pack at the candidate commit (absent)

findings.finding: The prerequisite-diagnostic scene states as fact that a lesson `PREM-LPP-001` exists and that a learner who fails the check should "revisit the dependency examples in `PREM-LPP-001`" or "return to that lesson's orientation and worked example." No lesson pack with that identifier exists anywhere in the repository at candidate commit 11c5b8b39e75cda2bc349c71758528f27fa0eacf; `lessons/` contains only this pack and `README.md`, and the project's own public map states the open lesson corpus is 0% complete. The prose therefore asserts unverifiable specifics (that the referenced pack contains dependency examples, an orientation scene, and a worked example) about content that does not exist, and it is the only recovery route offered for the two failure paths of the sole prerequisite check. `content/090-synthesis.md` correctly refers forward to the graph outcome `topic-orientation-mastery-and-retrieval` rather than to a pack ID, which shows the accurate pattern is already available in the pack.
findings.resolution: Replace the `PREM-LPP-001` pack references with the existing graph outcome identifier `topic-orientation-map-and-pathways` (already the declared prerequisite in `lesson.json`), or explicitly mark the pack as not yet published, and give a recovery route that a learner can actually execute at this commit — for example a bounded in-scene restatement of the node/prerequisite-link distinction plus a re-attempt with a fresh dependency chain.

findings.finding: The mapped claim states that recovery should reopen "the earliest **insecure** dependency that can account for the current gap," but the scene's stated criterion drops the insecurity condition — "Inspect the earliest dependency that may explain the current gap—Alpha in this route" — and presents Alpha as the single defensible answer for the chain Alpha -> Beta -> Gamma even though the stem supplies no evidence about Alpha or Beta. Root-first inspection is a defensible convention, but as written it also sits in tension with this lesson's own routing rule in `content/030-concept.md` ("choose the least support that still addresses the observed gap") and with its evidence-before-route model, since nothing has yet shown Alpha to be insecure.
findings.resolution: Align the scene wording with the mapped claim (e.g. "the earliest dependency not yet shown to be secure"), state the assumption that no evidence yet distinguishes Beta from Alpha, and acknowledge that probing the nearest prerequisite first is also defensible once evidence exists.

findings.finding: The operational definition of fragile knowledge lists only positive deficiency criteria (incomplete, inconsistent, cue-dependent, weakly explained, or unable to survive bounded variation). Several taught cases instead assign fragile on *absence* of discriminating evidence: Stage 1 practice ("has not yet tried a variation") is answered flatly as fragile, and Juniper in Stage 2 ("no explanation or variation") is answered as "fragile or unresolved." `content/030-concept.md` partly covers this with "Fragile is the conservative classification when the record mixes strong and weak evidence," but the glossary entry a learner is pointed to does not license inferring failed adaptation from an unattempted probe, and the two practice stages resolve the same evidential situation differently.
findings.resolution: Add the conservative-under-insufficient-evidence condition explicitly to `term-fragile-knowledge` (or introduce an `unresolved / insufficient evidence` designation), and make the Stage 1 and Stage 2 feedback treat unattempted-variation records consistently.

findings.finding: The synthesis table states the pattern requirement directly — "Secure | A pattern of accurate independent reasoning survives bounded variation" — but `claim-pattern-over-snapshot` does not list `scene-synthesis` in its `sceneIds`, so that scene's source note omits the claim under which the requirement is registered. The gap is not an evidence hole in practice: `claim-three-state-model` is mapped to the scene and defines the states by availability, accuracy, independence, and bounded adaptability, and the synthesis source note already lists all four supporting source IDs.
findings.resolution: Add `scene-synthesis` to `claim-pattern-over-snapshot.sceneIds` and name the claim in the synthesis `{source-note}`, or reword the table cell so the pattern requirement is clearly a restatement of `claim-three-state-model`.

findings.finding: Recalculated independently and confirmed correct: item points sum to 25 (2 + 3 + 10 + 10), each rubric's criteria sum exactly to its item's points (2; 2+1=3; 3+3+2+2=10; 4+2+2+2=10), the stated "20 of 25 overall with at least 6 of 10 on both open items" gate is arithmetically satisfiable and non-contradictory, `maxSelections: 2` matches the two-key answer for `item-separate-confidence`, and scene `estimatedMinutes` sum to exactly 120, matching both `lesson.json.estimatedMinutes` and the graph node's `estimatedHours: 2`.

All four answer keys were re-derived from the operational definitions and are correct: cue-dependent correct answer with a failed table variation supports fragile, not secure; varied accurate independent performance with low confidence supports provisional secure plus a calibration flag; and Cedar/Flint/Harbor map to unfamiliar/fragile/provisionally secure as keyed. No item rewards a scientifically false shortcut — the distractors that would be rewarded by a familiarity-only or confidence-only model are explicitly keyed wrong, and the open items require an uncertainty statement, so a learner cannot score well by asserting a permanent label.

Both were verified against their cited primary records rather than accepted on assertion. `site/data/premed-graph.json` contains `link-prerequisite-topic-orientation-map-and-pathways-topic-orientation-diagnostic-baseline` and `link-prerequisite-topic-orientation-diagnostic-baseline-topic-orientation-mastery-and-retrieval`, so the declared curriculum ordering is exact. `course/PREMED-SYLLABUS.md` states that confidence is logged before feedback and that a high-confidence incorrect response triggers misconception remediation, supporting the confidence-versus-performance separation at the declared scope. The guidance-fading asymmetry in `content/030-concept.md` matches the expertise-reversal evidence recorded in section 4 of the cited synthesis (high assistance d = 0.505 for low-prior-knowledge learners; d = -0.428 for high-prior-knowledge learners).

Boundary and uncertainty handling is a strength and was checked against the failure modes this role is asked to catch. The lesson excludes delayed retention, credit, admissions decisions, and any diagnosis of a learning difference or health condition; every classification is labelled provisional; and each claim records a scope and an uncertainty that stops short of psychometric validity, including the explicit statement that the three labels are not offered as a validated psychological taxonomy. No statement in the pack could reasonably be read as medical advice, clinical competence, or professional authorization.

limitations: This is one academic review run against one frozen candidate commit. It is a single eligible vote and establishes neither quorum nor merge readiness.

Verification was limited to the four repository-owned sources declared in references.json, each with agentAccess status checked-no-agent-restriction-found; no external literature was retrieved, so the meta-analytic effect sizes quoted in research/CONTENT-AUTHORING-EVIDENCE-2026-07-19.md were checked for faithful use by this lesson, not re-verified against the underlying primary studies.

The subject matter is learning-diagnostic decision logic rather than a natural-science domain, so there were no chemical equations, unit conversions, or numeric derivations to recalculate; quantitative checking was confined to assessment scoring, rubric point sums, and scene-time totals.

AUTHORING-BRIEF.md was deliberately not read, to avoid importing the authoring rationale into an independent review; the academic judgment rests only on learner-visible content and the structured pack artifacts.
Provenance recorded here is self-reported and attested by the accountable principal; nothing in this artifact proves that the declared provider, model, run identity, or instruction digest were used.
No claim is made about instructional effectiveness, learner outcomes, or accessibility conformance; those belong to the learning-design and accessibility-and-rights roles.
